new data centers being constructed to support artificial intelligence and other technology capabilities...Franklin Farms Power Station Units 1 and 2 are expected to be in service in 2028

limitations: AI investment intensity score reflects infrastructure investment to serve AI-driven data center customers, not Entergy's own internal AI capex; the company is an electricity provider, not an AI developer
Narrative concreteness reflects deployment of power infrastructure for named AI-customer data centers; Entergy's own internal AI use cases are not described in these excerpts
Multi-year capex totals for the full data center build-out program are not aggregated in a single figure in the excerpts, limiting precision on total program scale

Risk disclosure score of 4 reflects company-specific risk mechanisms (stranded assets, customer concentration, demand reduction from AI efficiency gains, cybersecurity threats from AI) with operational detail but no quantified risk dollar amounts or named governance body specifically for AI risk
